summary: Monster Other One, tight Garcia guitar and strong second‑set jams
blurb: The show delivers a monster "The Other One" jam, a soaring Space > The Other One sequence, and fiery first‑set numbers like "Red Rooster". Jerry’s guitar is front‑center throughout while Phil supplies an earthquake‑like bass line on the jams. Overall it’s a solid performance with clear instrument separation on the best soundboard source.
review: Feb 19 1982 at Golden Hall in San Diego captures a hot stretch of the Dead’s Southern California run. The first set rolls through roots staples – a scorching "Little Red Rooster" solo, punchy "Bertha", and the upbeat swing of "Greatest Story Ever Told" – while Brent’s keyboards sit nicely in the mix without drowning out the guitars. The second‑set is where the night truly shines: after an energetic "Lost Sailor > Saint Of Circumstance" the band launches into a thunderous pre‑drums jam that blossoms into Space, then explodes on a monster "The Other One" with Phil’s bass thundering five‑times through the theme. Jerry’s slide work and his “God‑like” tone dominate the climax, leading seamlessly into a soulful "Stella Blue" and the playful close of "Man Smart, Woman Smarter" → "Johnny B. Goode" before the encore’s rousing "U.S. Blues". The front‑of‑board recording (gd1982-02-19.fob.sonyecm220t.keshavan.miller.94395.sbeok.flac16) captures this energy with crystal‑clear instrument separation, making it the definitive source for this memorable night.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings are explicitly positive, praising the monster "The Other One", stellar Garcia guitar work, and inspired Space > The Other One sequence; two others are mixed but only note recording flaws, not performance problems. No consistent negative comments about playing appear, so a moderate positive adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Excellent front‑of‑board capture with clear instrument separation, crisp drums and Garcia’s guitar front‑center; reviewers call it the best‑sounding copy.

key_highlights: Monster "The Other One" jam with soaring post‑drums energy
Space > The Other One showcases inspired bass and Garcia’s guitar brilliance
Fiery first‑set roots numbers, especially a standout "Little Red Rooster" solo
Balanced keyboards and crisp drums keep the mix clean throughout

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers slide work and a "God‑like" tone on "Red Rooster", dominates the "Other One" jam with stellar guitar, and stays front‑center in the mix.
band_performance.Phil: Lesch provides an exuberant "earthquake" riff during the Other One jam, his bass is clear and thunderous—especially notable on Space > The Other One.
band_performance.Bob: Weir’s rhythm work shines on "Greatest Story Ever Told"; his slide on "El Paso" is highlighted despite some vocal mix issues.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboards are well balanced, offering supportive texture without swallowing the guitars or vocals.
band_performance.Mickey: Hart’s drumming is crisp and clearly defined throughout, with strong pre‑drums playing and solid support in post‑drums jams.
band_performance.Billy: Kreutzmann delivers tight, articulate drums that complement Hart; both drummers are praised for clear definition.
